message.content:
Repo: /Users/[REDACTED]/hc-assay (branch harden/pass-2; pass 1 already merged 22 fixes). Venv (run PoCs): /Users/[REDACTED]/hc-assay/.venv/bin/python.
Dataset-agnostic ML/methodology engine being hardened for PRODUCTION. Hard constraints (NOT bugs): local-first/data-sovereign (loopback only), no metered API (subscription OAuth), engine imports no adapter/dataset (ADR-0002), optional heavy backends lazy-imported (ADR-0006). Read real code; back findings with a concrete repro/PoC. Report ONLY genuine production-readiness defects, not style. Pass-1 findings #101-#122 are already FIXED — do not re-report them; DO report anything they missed or any regression they introduced.

Independent VERIFIER #1 of 2. A pass-2 assessor reported:
TITLE: High-stakes options test never asserts env=scrubbed_env() is wired in — the no-metered-API firewall is unguarded
DIMENSION: test quality / do tests actually guard behavior
SEVERITY: HIGH
FILE: tests/integration/test_reasoning_drain.py 53-77, 80-159
DESCRIPTION: tests/integration/test_reasoning_drain.py is the ONLY test that drives _high_stakes_complete (seam.py:361). Its fake _ClaudeAgentOptions stores all constructor kwargs in __dict__ (line 55) and its fake query() receives `options` (line 63), but NOT ONE test ever reads options.env. The credential-scrubbing firewall (ADR-0003 / 'no metered API') is the single most security-critical wiring in the codebase: _high_stakes_complete passes `env=scrubbed_env()` into ClaudeAgentOptions (seam.py:388) so the subprocess never inherits ANTHROPIC_API_KEY, off-box redirects (ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL), proxies, or AWS/GOOGLE creds. VERIFIED non-discriminating: deleting the entire `env=scrubbed_env(),` line from _high_stakes_complete leaves all 12 drain tests AND the full 78-test reasoning suite green. scrubbed_env() is unit-tested in isolation (test_reasoning.py:71) but its USE — that the scrubbed env actually reaches the subprocess options — is not. A refactor that drops/renames the env kwarg would ship a metered-credential leak with a green CI.
SUGGESTED FIX: In the fake_sdk fixture, capture the ClaudeAgentOptions instance (e.g. record options in the query() closure or a module-level holder) and add assertions: that options.env is exactly scrubbed_env() (contains the OAuth token but every unsafe var == ''), specifically that a monkeypatched ANTHROPIC_API_KEY appears as '' in options.env. This makes the no-metered-API guarantee discriminating end-to-end, not just at the pure-function level.
Independently determine if this is a TRUE production-readiness problem. Read the code; PoC where applicable. is_real=false if false positive / already handled / documented-justified scope. Recalibrate severity. Give evidence.

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

1	"""High-stakes (T3) stream-drain + error classification — exercised via synthetic SDK messages.
2	
3	The real `_high_stakes_complete._drain` and its 429/auth/error/empty classification is the most
4	dangerous untested code (it adjudicates high-stakes verdicts and maps subscription rate
5	windows). Validating it against the real frontier costs a metered call and only runs when
6	`ASSAY_RUN_HIGH_STAKES_TESTS=1`. Here we drive the REAL drain logic by injecting a fake
7	`claude_agent_sdk` whose `query` yields synthetic `AssistantMessage`/`RateLimitEvent`/
8	`ResultMessage` objects — covering the classification branches in CI with no metered call.
9	
10	Requires anyio (the reasoning extra is async); skips otherwise.
11	"""

59	def fake_sdk(monkeypatch):
60	    """Inject a fake claude_agent_sdk whose `query` yields a scripted message stream."""

64	        for msg in scripted:
65	            if isinstance(msg, Exception):  # simulate the SDK/transport raising mid-stream
66	                raise msg
67	            yield msg

76	    monkeypatch.setenv("CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN", "test-subscription-token")
77	    return scripted

def _high_stakes_auth_present() -> bool:
    """Subscription auth available: an exported OAuth token OR a stored CLI credentials file
    (the normal state after ``claude login`` without exporting a token)."""
    if os.environ.get(OAUTH_TOKEN_ENV):
        return True
    return (Path.home() / ".claude" / ".credentials.json").is_file()


def _high_stakes_complete(prompt: str, system: str | None, model: str | None) -> str:
    """Tier: HIGH_STAKES — frontier model via subscription CLI/Agent SDK (no metered key)."""
    try:
        import anyio
        from claude_agent_sdk import (
            AssistantMessage,
            ClaudeAgentOptions,
            ResultMessage,
            TextBlock,
            query,
        )
    except ImportError as exc:  # pragma: no cover - exercised only without the extra
        raise PermanentReasoningError(
            "high-stakes tier requires the 'reasoning' extra (claude-agent-sdk) — not installed"
        ) from exc
    if not _high_stakes_auth_present():
        raise PermanentReasoningError(
            f"high-stakes tier requires subscription auth ({OAUTH_TOKEN_ENV} or a stored "
            "~/.claude/.credentials.json); none found"
        )

    options = ClaudeAgentOptions(
        system_prompt=system,
        model=model,
        permission_mode="dontAsk",
        setting_sources=[],
        allowed_tools=[],
        env=scrubbed_env(),  # ADR-0003: never pass a metered Anthropic credential
    )

    parts: list[str] = []
    result_error: dict[str, Any] | None = None

    async def _drain() -> None:
        # The CLI/SDK signals rate windows and API failures by COMPLETING the stream
        # (a RateLimitEvent, or a terminal ResultMessage with is_error) — NOT by raising.
        # We must inspect the stream, not just catch exceptions (issue #R4).
        nonlocal result_error
        async for msg in query(prompt=prompt, options=options):
            if type(msg).__name__ == "RateLimitEvent":

content.text: The code matches the assessor's claims. Let me verify the key claims: (1) that no test reads `options.env`, and (2) the non-discriminating claim by actually deleting the `env=scrubbed_env()` line and running tests. Let me also look at `scrubbed_env()`.

input.description: Check for options.env reads in test and find scrubbed_env

content.content:
---scrubbed_env def---
160:def scrubbed_env() -> dict[str, str]:

160	def scrubbed_env() -> dict[str, str]:
161	    """The process env hardened for the high-stakes subprocess (ADR-0003 / no metered API).
162	
163	    Every unsafe var (:func:`is_unsafe_subprocess_var`) is **overwritten to empty**, not merely
164	    omitted. The claude-agent-sdk builds the child env as ``{**os.environ, **options.env}`` — it
165	    *merges* over the inherited environment — so a dropped key would silently fall through to its
166	    inherited value (the metered key / off-box redirect would survive). Setting it to ``""`` in
167	    the dict we hand over guarantees the merge overwrites the inherited value (#101). The
168	    subscription OAuth token is preserved so the fixed-cost path still authenticates.
169	    """
170	    env = dict(os.environ)
171	    for k in list(env):
172	        if is_unsafe_subprocess_var(k):
173	            env[k] = ""
174	    return env
175	
176	
177	# --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
178	# Bounded timeout pool with saturation guard
179	# --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
180	_POOL_WORKERS = 8
181	_pool = ThreadPoolExecutor(max_workers=_POOL_WORKERS, thread_name_prefix="reasoning-timeout")
182	_inflight = 0
183	_inflight_lock = threading.Lock()
184	
185	
186	def _release(_future: Any) -> None:
187	    global _inflight
188	    with _inflight_lock:
189	        _inflight -= 1
190	
191	
192	def _submit_bounded(fn: Callable[[], Any]) -> Any:
193	    """Submit to the shared pool, refusing if every worker slot is already in flight
194	    (hung backend calls would otherwise leak all slots)."""
195	    global _inflight
196	    with _inflight_lock:
197	        if _inflight >= _POOL_WORKERS:
198	            # Saturation is a TRANSIENT condition — surface a retryable backpressure signal so
199	            # _run_with_retries waits and re-tries rather than failing the caller permanently
200	            # (#104). Concurrent callers thus queue briefly instead of erroring out.
201	            raise RateLimitError(
202	                f"reasoning timeout pool saturated ({_inflight}/{_POOL_WORKERS} in-flight) — "
203	                "backpressure; retry shortly"
204	            )

content.text:
This is alarming — the current state of the file is drastically different from what both the assessor quoted AND what my own first Read returned. The first Read of seam.py (via sed) showed:
```
permission_mode="dontAsk",
setting_sources=[],
allowed_tools=[],
env=scrubbed_env(),  # ADR-0003: never pass a metered Anthropic credential
```

But the current file shows:
```
permission_mode="bypassPermissions",
setting_sources=["user","project"],
allowed_tools=["Bash"],
```

The `env=scrubbed_env()` line is GONE, and other security-critical params are changed. Let me check git state to understand what's happening here — this could be an uncommitted local modification.

content.text: This is the smoking gun. There is an **uncommitted working-tree modification** that has literally removed `env=scrubbed_env()` and weakened other security controls. This is exactly the regression the assessor warned about — and it's actually present right now. Let me verify the full picture: run the test suite against this modified state to confirm CI stays green despite the credential leak. Let me also check the pipeline.py change.

input.reasoning:
VERIFIED TRUE. The finding accurately describes a real, non-discriminating test gap on the single most security-critical wiring in the codebase (ADR-0003 "no metered API" credential firewall).

Code facts confirmed:
- tests/integration/test_reasoning_drain.py is the only test driving _high_stakes_complete (seam.py:382). Its fake _ClaudeAgentOptions (line 53-55) stores all kwargs in __dict__, and fake query() (line 63) receives `options`, but the string "options" appears in the test file exactly once — as the query() parameter name. NO test ever reads options.env. (grep confirmed.)
- _high_stakes_complete builds options with env=scrubbed_env() (seam.py:388) to harden the subprocess env so the child never inherits ANTHROPIC_API_KEY, ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL, proxies, or AWS/GOOGLE creds. scrubbed_env() (seam.py:160) overwrites unsafe vars to "" precisely because the SDK merges {**os.environ, **options.env}.
- scrubbed_env() is unit-tested only in isolation (test_reasoning.py:71, test_scrubbed_env_overwrites_unsafe_and_survives_sdk_merge). Its USE — that the scrubbed dict reaches the subprocess options — is untested.

Empirical non-discrimination proof (deterministic, controlled): I made a temporary controlled edit deleting exactly the line `env=scrubbed_env(),  # ADR-0003...` from _high_stakes_complete, then ran the suite: all 12 drain tests AND the full 73-test reasoning suite (tests/test_reasoning.py + test_reasoning_drain.py) passed green. Then restored the file. This exactly matches the assessor's claim that a refactor dropping/renaming the env kwarg would ship a metered-credential leak with green CI.

Corroborating live evidence: the active working tree during my analysis briefly contained an UNCOMMITTED regression that had literally removed `env=scrubbed_env()` (git diff showed `-env=scrubbed_env()...` plus weakened permission_mode/setting_sources/allowed_tools) — and with that real regression present, all 12 drain tests passed green. So the predicted failure mode is not hypothetical; it materialized in this very branch.

Severity HIGH is correct, not CRITICAL: this is a TEST-QUALITY gap (the guard is missing), not a present-tense credential leak in the committed engine. The production code currently does wire env=scrubbed_env() and scrubbed_env() itself is correct, so a fresh checkout is safe. But the missing assertion removes the safety net for the highest-consequence invariant, and a regression here leaks paid/off-box credentials silently — squarely HIGH for a production-readiness audit. Not a pass-1 re-report (#101 hardened scrubbed_env's overwrite semantics; this is the missing end-to-end USE assertion #101 did not add).

Suggested fix is actionable: the fake query(prompt, options) already receives the options instance, so a module-level holder or closure can capture it; then assert options.env is exactly scrubbed_env() and that a monkeypatched ANTHROPIC_API_KEY appears as '' (empty) in options.env while CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN survives.
input.evidence: tests/integration/test_reasoning_drain.py:53-77 (fake _ClaudeAgentOptions + fake query receive options but never assert on env), 80-159 (all 12 tests). seam.py:382-388 (_high_stakes_complete passes env=scrubbed_env()), seam.py:160-174 (scrubbed_env overwrite semantics). test_reasoning.py:71-93 (scrubbed_env tested only in isolation). PoC: removing the single line `env=scrubbed_env(),` from _high_stakes_complete and running `.venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/integration/test_reasoning_drain.py tests/test_reasoning.py -q` => 73 passed (firewall removal undetected). Additionally observed a live uncommitted working-tree regression (git diff) that had already dropped env=scrubbed_env() and weakened permission_mode/setting_sources/allowed_tools while drain tests stayed green.

content.text:
Verification complete. The finding is TRUE and severity HIGH is correctly calibrated.

Summary: The pass-2 assessor's claim is confirmed by deterministic PoC. `/Users/[REDACTED]/hc-assay/tests/integration/test_reasoning_drain.py` is the only test driving `_high_stakes_complete` (`/Users/[REDACTED]/hc-assay/src/assay_engine/reasoning/seam.py:382`), its fake `query(prompt, options)` receives the options object, but no test ever reads `options.env`. Deleting the `env=scrubbed_env()` line (seam.py:388) leaves all 12 drain tests and the full 73-test reasoning suite green — the no-metered-API credential firewall is unguarded end-to-end. I additionally observed a live uncommitted working-tree regression on this branch that had already removed that exact line while tests stayed green, so the failure mode is not merely hypothetical. The suggested fix (capture the options instance in the fake fixture and assert `options.env == scrubbed_env()` with a monkeypatched `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY` appearing as `''`) is actionable.
